div.p: Slope Analysis The secant line through (x-h, f(x-h)) and (x+h, f(x+h)) approximates the tangent at x

The Central Difference uses points on both sides of x, meaning the first-order error terms cancel out in the subtraction. This results in an approximation that is orders of magnitude more stable than Forward or Backward differences.
Order: O(h²) Taylor expansion cancels the O(h) term: Error ≈ (h²/6)f'''(ξ)
Order: O(ε/h) Floating-point noise ε is amplified by small h. Limit: 1e-16 for double.
h_opt ≈ √ε ≈ 1e-8 Minimizes combined Total Error function.
